computation_name: Graph Kernel Sketches and Random Feature Embeddings
definition: Approximate expensive graph similarity by converting each graph into explicit WL feature histograms, hash graph-kernel features, random graph embeddings, or node-distribution sketches, then compute vector similarity between graph-level features.

scale_profile.best_for: Collections of many graphs where exact graph kernels, graph edit distance, or all-pairs node alignment is too expensive.
scale_profile.typical_upper_bound: Thousands to millions of graphs depending on graph size and feature dimensionality.
scale_profile.avoid_when: The audit requires exact graph edit distance and no approximation validation is possible.

tunable_parameters.name: wl_iterations
tunable_parameters.purpose: Controls local neighborhood depth for WL features.

tunable_parameters.name: random_feature_count
tunable_parameters.purpose: Controls approximation variance for random graph embeddings.

tunable_parameters.name: feature_hash_size
tunable_parameters.purpose: Controls memory and collision rate for hash graph kernels.

quality_gates: Compare approximate graph similarities with exact graph kernel or edit distance on a subset.
Report node/edge labels, WL depth, and feature hashing settings.
Check that graph-size effects are not the only driver of similarity.
failure_modes: WL features can miss structural differences beyond the selected depth.
Feature hashing collisions can distort rare substructures.
Graph-size normalization choices can dominate SPECTRA curves.
example_usage: Export each graph's WL or substructure counts as weighted feature strings, then run weighted_jaccard_topk.py to emit pairwise graph similarities.

citations.title: Scalable Global Alignment Graph Kernel Using Random Features
citations.evidence: Uses random graph embeddings to approximate global graph kernels and reduce complexity from quadratic to near-linear in the number of graphs.

citations.title: Faster Kernels for Graphs with Continuous Attributes via Hashing
citations.evidence: Introduces hash graph kernels that use LSH to apply scalable graph kernels to continuously attributed graphs.
